Journal Observatory Dashboard

Use of Visualizations for Continuous Data

Global Aggregated Trend

About this Dashboard

This dashboard tracks how data-visualisation practices in scientific publishing have changed over time. The central question is simple but consequential: are researchers increasingly choosing more informative ways to display their results?

Data are organised across 12 Research Fields:

  • CARDIAC & CARDIOVASCULAR SYSTEMS
  • CLINICAL NEUROLOGY
  • ENDOCRINOLOGY & METABOLISM
  • GENETICS & HEREDITY
  • IMMUNOLOGY
  • NEUROSCIENCES
  • ONCOLOGY
  • ORTHOPEDICS
  • PHARMACOLOGY & PHARMACY
  • PHYSIOLOGY
  • RHEUMATOLOGY
  • UROLOGY & NEPHROLOGY

For each field you can explore an aggregated trend across all journals in the field and a per-journal breakdown with one chart per journal. A GLOBAL tab provides a cross-field overview.

What the Metrics Mean

% Bars

The percentage of papers that year that included at least one bar chart. Bar charts are widely used but criticised for hiding the underlying data distribution — a tall bar can represent two data points or two hundred.

% Informative Graphs

The percentage of papers that year that used a more transparent alternative — such as box plots, violin plots, dot plots, or strip charts — that expose the shape and spread of the underlying data.

Policy Year Marker (per-journal plots)

A solid vertical line marking the year a journal adopted an editorial recommendation on figure types. Use it to assess visually whether the policy was followed by a change in author behaviour.

Policy Adoption Distribution (aggregated plots)

On aggregated plots, semi-transparent yellow bands replace the single vline. Each band’s width is proportional to the share of journals that adopted a policy that year — a thick band means many journals acted at once; a thin band marks an isolated early or late mover.

Navigating the Dashboard

Sidebar

The left sidebar lists all available views. Click ℹ About to return to this page, GLOBAL for the cross-field overview, or any Research Field name to open that field’s dedicated panel. The active view is highlighted.

GLOBAL tab

Shows the overall aggregated trend across every journal in the dataset, followed by a grid of per-field aggregated charts so you can compare fields side by side. The column slider controls how many field charts appear per row.

Per-field tabs

Each Research Field tab contains two sections:

  • Aggregated Trend — a single chart averaging all journals in the field, with policy-adoption distribution bands.
  • Individual Journal Trends — one chart per journal, each with its own policy-year marker, arranged in a resizable grid.

Interactive Controls

Column slider

The Columns slider (above each journal grid) sets how many charts appear per row, from 1 up to 6. Drag it left for a single large view or right to see more journals simultaneously. Chart titles and y-axis margins automatically synchronise so all charts in a row remain perfectly aligned even when title lengths differ.

Journal search

Type in the Find journal box to filter the grid: non-matching cards fade out so you can spot the journal you want at a glance. Selecting a suggestion from the dropdown (or pressing Enter after typing a full name) scrolls directly to that card and briefly highlights it with a flash animation. Click ✕ to clear the search and restore all cards.

Drag-to-reorder

Each journal card has a narrow ⠿ grip strip along its left edge. Drag it to move that card to any position within the grid — useful for placing two journals you want to compare side by side. The target card is highlighted as you drag so you can see exactly where it will land.

Global legend toggles

The toolbar above all panels contains three toggle buttons:

  • % Bars — show or hide the bar-chart metric in every plot on the page simultaneously.
  • % Informative Graphs — same for the informative-graph metric.
  • Policy adoption — show or hide the policy-year lines and bands in every plot at once. Useful when you want a clean view of the trend lines without the policy overlay.

A struck-through button indicates that layer is currently hidden; click again to restore it.

Per-chart legend

Inside each chart, click a legend entry to show or hide that specific line in that chart only. Double-click an entry to isolate it (hiding all other lines); double-click again to restore all. This works independently of the global toggles above.

Chart Interactions (Plotly)

Every chart is interactive. Hover over a data point to see a tooltip with the exact values for that year. Additional interactions are available via mouse:

🔍

Zoom

Click and drag on the chart area to zoom into a time range. Double-click anywhere on the chart to reset the zoom.

✋

Pan

After zooming in, click and drag while holding Shift to pan across the time axis.

↩

Reset view

Double-click anywhere inside the plot area to return to the default zoom level and axis ranges.
